title: Just watched NGE no movie death and rebirth for 1st time
display_title: Just watched NGE no movie death and rebirth for 1st time
wikitext:
[]: Just watched NGE (no movie/death and rebirth) for 1st time             Quote Post by alih92 » Thu Jan 21, 2010 4:58 pm What should I watch now? Also, I watched NGE, with subs and I'm totally confused and let down by the ending.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:17 pm man this is awesome, there was someone who was, uhh, virgin regarding nature of EoTV? That's becoming excessively rare! In any case: Watch End of Evangelion right now. Xard :)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:21 pm Correct: Renewal End of Evangelion fansub, watch that, and enjoy!

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:29 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Correct: Renewal End of Evangelion fansub, watch that, and enjoy!

[]: Re: Just watched NGE (no movie/death and rebirth) for 1st ti             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:30 pm alih92 wrote: I'm totally confused and let down by the ending.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:31 pm CONGRATULATIONS! ...how can you forget the climax, FFF?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:33 pm Haha! I didn't, and I don't. I actually do find myself enjoying the ending every so often.

[]:             Quote Post by alih92 » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:45 pm Yeah I'm amazed too, I've seen lots of "community considered" anime "classics". I've seen the following: Akira Ghost In the Shell Original Movie :) Outlaw Star Trigun YuYu Hakusho FLCL Cowboy Bebop Wolf's Rain Death Note Code Geass Hellsing Full Metal Alchemist but I've never gotten around to watching Neon Genesis. I have to say that so far my favorite anime's have been a tie between Death Note, Full Metal Alchemist, and Neon Genesis. I have no idea but what is it about Death Note, it's the new NGE...? Should I watch death and rebirth? Lastly, is there a difference between End of Evangelion and Renewal End of Evangelion? Oh and I watched episodes 20-24 (directors cut) not the originals.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:47 pm alih92 wrote: I have no idea but what is it about Death Note, it's the new NGE...?

[]: Re: Just watched NGE (no movie/death and rebirth) for 1st ti             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:48 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Haha! Not expecting the slide show, huh? *click* Shinji: This is a picture of me questioning my existence. *click* Shinji: This is another picture of me questioning my existence. The chair was kinda uncomfortable. *click* Shinji: This is me wondering what the f-'s going on... *click* Shinji: Oh, look! Some animation from a more enjoyable show! See how happy I am?

[]:             Quote Post by alih92 » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:50 pm Xard wrote: alih92 wrote: I have no idea but what is it about Death Note, it's the new NGE...?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:53 pm >atleast until the 2nd arc of the story. exactly

[]:             Quote Post by alih92 » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:54 pm true, but can't the same be said for NGE, that it's praised for character development and its link to philosphical/psychological subjects but when viewers get to the ending for the first time...yeah

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:56 pm alih92 wrote: true, but can't the same be said for NGE, that it's praised for character development and its link to philosphical/psychological subjects but when viewers get to the ending for the first time...yeah

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:57 pm End of Evangelion is the next logical step. As has been mentioned Death and Rebirth is to some extent... superfluous, and can be skipped with no ill effect. Personally, I think that before watching EoE however, if you haven't already done so, make sure you have seen the Director's Cut versions of episodes #21-#24, as these contain ties ins to EoE. In fact, even if you have, watch these four episodes again anyway before moving onto the main feature. The EoE specific backstory is at its most dense in these four episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:58 pm By any chance did you make sure to watch the director's cut versions of 21-24? As for Death, watch the renewal version if you want but there isn't anything that significant that wasn't added to the director's cut episodes. Except for that whole Kaworu in Rei 1's eye thing...lol Anno

[]:             Quote Post by alih92 » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:58 pm so, what other animes are similar to Death Note or Neon Genesis? And why am I so attached to the character of Rei? oh and yes I watched 21-24 directors cuts :)

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Jan 21, 2010 5:59 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Personally, I think that before watching EoE however, if you haven;t already done so, make sure you have seen the Director's Cut versions of episodes #21-#24, as these contain ties ins to EoE. In fact, even if you have, watch these four episodes again anyway before moving onto the main feature. The EoE specific backstory is at its most dense in these four episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Jan 21, 2010 6:02 pm alih92 wrote: but when viewers get to the ending for the first time...yeah

[]:             Quote Post by alih92 » Thu Jan 21, 2010 7:07 pm guys anything similar to death note or nge?
